name: Copernicium
name_origin: Named after Polish astronomer Nicolaus Copernicus, who proposed the heliocentric model of the solar system.

discovery.discoverer: Gesellschaft fur Schwerionenforschung (GSI) team led by Sigurd Hofmann

occurrence.abundance_summary: Copernicium does not occur naturally. It is a synthetic element produced in particle accelerators via reactions such as 70Zn + 208Pb.
states: synthetic, produced atom-by-atom in particle accelerators

reactivity_summary: Gas-phase chemistry experiments indicate that copernicium is a volatile metal with mercury-like adsorption behavior on gold surfaces, consistent with group 12 chemistry but with strong relativistic effects on the closed 7s2 shell that make it less reactive than mercury and reminiscent of a noble gas. No bulk compounds have been isolated.
isotopes_summary: All copernicium isotopes are radioactive. The longest-lived confirmed isotope is 285Cn with a half-life of about 30 seconds; this isotope has been the workhorse for chemical investigations because it is produced as a decay product of flerovium isotopes.

citations.source: IUPAC Periodic Table of the Elements
Royal Society of Chemistry - Periodic Table: Copernicium
Eichler, R. et al. 'Chemical characterization of element 112', Nature, 2007
